channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: No ICD, No Deal: India Draws Red Line in Mega Rafale Fighter Negotiations
item.description: India’s ambitious ?3.25 lakh crore Medium Role Fighter Aircraft (MRFA) program for the acquisition of 114 Rafale fighter jets has reached a stage, with access to the Interface Control Document (ICD) emerging as the central issue that could determine the fate of the deal.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Lockheed Martin Positions S-92 Helicopter for India’s VVIP Transport Role, Highlights Proven Safety and Tata Partnership
item.description: Lockheed Martin has signaled that its S-92 helicopter remains a strong contender for potential VVIP and Head of State transport requirements in India, emphasizing the platform’s global track record, safety credentials, and deep industrial linkage with India through Tata.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India to get Rafale F5 with 6 Meteor Missiles as Upgraded M88 T-REX Engine Aims to Offset Drag Penalties
item.description: The upcoming Rafale F5 standard is expected to significantly enhance beyond-visual-range (BVR) combat capability by increasing its Meteor missile carriage from the current maximum of four to potentially six. This expansion leverages the use of additional wing wet hardpoints, a configuration that has long been technically feasible but operationally constrained by aerodynamic drag and performance trade-offs.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: KF-21 Boramae vs Rafale F4: Can South Korea’s New Fighter Outcompete France in India’s MRFA Deal?
item.description: As India revives momentum around its long-pending 114-aircraft MRFA (Multi-Role Fighter Aircraft) program, a new contender is quietly entering the strategic conversation: South Korea’s KF-21 Boramae, particularly the upcoming Block II variant. With both India and South Korea aggressively pursuing defence exports, the possibility of Seoul offering the KF-21 under a “Make in India” framework raises a critical question—does it make more sense than the battle-proven Rafale F4 for India’s future force structure?
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO Hands Over ‘Prajna’ AI Satellite Intelligence System to MHA, Boosting India’s Real-Time Internal Security Capabilities
item.description: India has taken a significant step toward strengthening its internal security architecture with the formal induction of the AI-enabled satellite imaging system ‘Prajna’. The system was handed over by the Secretary, Department of Defence R&D (DDR&D), Ministry of Defence, to the Union Home Secretary at a ceremony held at Kartavya Bhawan-3 under the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA). Developed by DRDO’s Centre for Artificial Intelligence and Robotics (CAIR), Prajna is designed to provide real-time decision support to security agencies, marking a shift from conventional surveillance frameworks to intelligent, AI-driven situational awareness.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Chinese Satellite imagery Exposes Pakistan’s Defence Shortcomings in Operation Sindoor
item.description: In the heat of Operation Sindoor, when India launched decisive strikes against terror infrastructure following the horrific Pahalgam attack, Pakistan turned desperately to its all-weather friend China for critical backing. Beijing reportedly stepped in with real-time intelligence inputs, repositioned satellite coverage focused on Indian movements, and even assisted in reorganizing Pakistan’s radar and air defence networks to counter the impending Indian response.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Sherp SMV N1200 Now Made in India: The World’s Most Extreme Off-Roader Rolls Out of Chandigarh
item.description: When it comes to off-road capability, most vehicles pride themselves on conquering sand dunes, rocky trails, or snow-covered terrain. But the Sherp SMV N1200 operates in a completely different league—one where conventional off-roaders simply stop. Now, this extraordinary machine is being manufactured in India, marking a significant milestone for niche mobility platforms in the country.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: MoD inks Rs 975 crore contracts for TRAWL Assembly for T-72/T-90 Tanks to enhance Indian Army’s minefield breaching capability
item.description: The Ministry of Defence has signed contracts with Bharat Earth Movers Limited (BEML) and Electro Pneumatics and Hydraulics (India) Private Limited for the procurement of TRAWL Assembly for T-72/T-90 Tanks, at an approximate cost of Rs 975 crore. The contracts were inked in the presence of Defence Secretary Shri Rajesh Kumar Singh in New Delhi on April 21, 2026.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh flies into Germany in A400M, Get Royal welcome with escort of fighter jets
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh arrived in Germany on a three-day visit . The minister was received at the Munich airport by Indian Ambassador Ajit Gupte. He arrived in Berlin from Munich in a special German Air Force plane and was escorted by fighter jets as a pilot, a gesture of respect from Germany towards India.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Some Boundaries Should Never Be Crossed: Army Reiterates Resolve Ahead Of Pahalgam Attack Anniv
item.description: Ahead of the anniversary of the Pahalgam terror attack, the Indian Army on Tuesday reaffirmed its commitment to justice and warned of a decisive response if the “boundaries of humanity” are crossed.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Drone Training Centre For NCC Cadets In Nagpur Soon, Says Top Official
item.description: Additional Director General of the NCC Directorate Maharashtra, Major General Vivek Tyagi, said on Monday that a drone training centre will come up in Nagpur soon for NCC Cadets.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Japan opens door to global arms market with overhaul of defence export rules
item.description: Japan on ?Tuesday unveiled its biggest overhaul of defence export rules in decades, scrapping restrictions on overseas arms sales and ?opening the way for exports of warships, missiles and other weapons.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pahalgam Attack: Victim’s Wife Vows To Dedicate Life To Nation, Religion
item.description: Marking one year since the tragic Pahalgam terror attack, Pragati Jagdale, wife of Santosh Jagdale, one of the 26 victims brutally gunned down, highlighted her enduring trauma and her resolve to dedicate her life to the nation and her faith.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India in touch with Iran for safe exit of ships via Strait of Hormuz: MEA
item.description: India is in touch with the Iranian authorities for the safety of its ships and for their safe exit through the Strait of Hormuz, the Ministry of External Affairs said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, UN officials discuss peacekeeping cooperation during meeting
item.description: Issues related to United Nations peacekeeping were among the key topics discussed during a meeting between senior Indian and UN officials here, the Permanent Mission of India to the United Nations said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: admin
